title: FinitePart
summary: Defines the finite part as the constant Laurent coefficient and the log anomaly as the logarithmic coefficient.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines the finite part as the constant Laurent coefficient and the log anomaly as the logarithmic coefficient.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Renormalization/FinitePart.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The constant Laurent coefficient. In explicit terms, for r a type, and e of type laurent log expansion r, its defining expression is E.coeff 0.
correspondence.leanType: def finitePart {R : Type u} [Zero R] (E : LaurentLogExpansion R) : R

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The coefficient of log(epsilon). In explicit terms, for r a type, and e of type laurent log expansion r, its defining expression is E.log Coeff.
correspondence.leanType: def logAnomaly {R : Type u} [Zero R] (E : LaurentLogExpansion R) : R

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The assertion that an expansion contains no logarithmic anomaly. In explicit terms, for r a type, and e of type laurent log expansion r, its defining expression is E.log Coeff equals 0.
correspondence.leanType: def HasNoLogTerm {R : Type u} [Zero R] (E : LaurentLogExpansion R) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For R a type, and E of type Laurent Log Expansion R, finite Part E equals E.coeff 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem finitePart_eq_coeff_zero {R : Type u} [Zero R] (E : LaurentLogExpansion R) : finitePart E = E.coeff 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For R a type, and E of type Laurent Log Expansion R, log Anomaly E equals E.log Coeff.
correspondence.leanType: theorem logAnomaly_eq_logCoeff {R : Type u} [Zero R] (E : LaurentLogExpansion R) : logAnomaly E = E.logCoeff

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: This file establishes syntax, aggregate imports, or an abstract bridge. It must not be read as a derivation of the corresponding geometric theorem from concrete tensor and analytic data.
